Вот промпт для Cursor под фронтенд:

---

**Build the complete frontend for a wholesale order management web application.**

**Tech stack:** HTML5 + CSS3 + vanilla JavaScript only. No frameworks, no npm, no build tools. All in static files served by Flask via Jinja2 templates.

**Project structure for frontend:**
```
/templates
  base.html          — base layout with nav
  login.html         — login screen
  order_form.html    — main order page (catalogue + cart)
  order_history.html — client's past orders
  manager_dashboard.html — manager order list
/static
  /css
    style.css
  /js
    api.js           — all fetch() calls to REST API
    order_form.js    — order form logic
    manager.js       — manager dashboard logic
```

**Color scheme and visual style:**
- White background `#FFFFFF`
- Primary dark blue `#1F3864`
- Secondary blue `#2E5496`
- Light blue accent `#EEF4FB`
- Success green `#2E7D32` / background `#E8F5E9`
- Error red `#C62828` / background `#FDECEA`
- Warning amber `#7B5A00` / background `#FFF3CD`
- Font: system-ui or Segoe UI, 14px base
- Minimal design — no shadows, no gradients, flat

**Pages to build:**

---

**1. login.html**

Simple centered card on white background. Fields: login (text), password (password). Button: "Log In" — dark blue. On error show red message below button. On success redirect to /order. No registration link.

```javascript
// On submit:
POST /api/login
body: { login, password }
response: { token, role } // role = 'client' or 'manager'
// Store token in sessionStorage
// If role = 'manager' → redirect to /manager
// If role = 'client' → redirect to /order
```

---

**2. order_form.html**

Two-column layout. Left column (60%): product catalogue. Right column (40%): cart summary + submit.

**Left — product catalogue:**
- Top section "Frequently ordered" — up to 5 items highlighted with light blue background, loaded first
- Below: full product list
- Each product row: name, category, unit, stock available, price, quantity input (number, min=0, step=0.01)
- If quantity input > stock_qty → highlight input red, show "Only X available" tooltip
- Products with stock_qty = 0 → greyed out, quantity input disabled

```javascript
// On page load:
GET /api/products
// Returns array sorted by recommendation score first, then alphabetically
// response: [{ id_product, name, category, unit, stock_qty, price, freq }]
// freq > 0 → show in "Frequently ordered" section
```

**Right — cart summary:**
- Live-updating list of items where quantity > 0
- Each cart row: name, quantity, unit, line total (qty * price)
- Order total at bottom
- Delivery date picker (required, min = tomorrow)
- Comment textarea (optional, max 500 chars)
- Button "Submit Order" — dark blue, full width
- Button "Clear" — text-only, grey

```javascript
// On submit:
POST /api/orders
body: {
  date_delivery: "YYYY-MM-DD",
  comment: "...",
  lines: [{ id_product, quantity }]  // only items where quantity > 0
}

// Response if confirmed:
{ status: "confirmed", id_order, date_delivery }
→ Show green confirmation banner, clear cart, keep form

// Response if deficit:
{ status: "deficit", deficit_lines: [
    { id_product, name, requested, available,
      alternatives: [{ id_product, name, stock_qty, price }] }
  ]
}
→ Show deficit modal
```

**Deficit modal:**
- Overlay modal, not closable by clicking outside
- Title: "Some items are unavailable"
- Table with deficit items: name | requested | available | alternatives dropdown
- Each deficit row: dropdown to select an alternative or "Remove this line"
- Button "Update order and resubmit" → update quantities in form, close modal, auto-resubmit
- Button "Cancel order" → close modal, reset form

---

**3. order_history.html**

Simple table layout.

```javascript
// On load:
GET /api/orders
// Returns: [{ id_order, date_created, date_delivery, status, total }]
```

Table columns: Order #, Date placed, Delivery date, Status (coloured badge), Total. Clicking a row expands it inline to show order lines:

```javascript
GET /api/orders/:id
// Returns: { ...order, lines: [{ name, quantity, unit, price_fixed, line_total }] }
```

Status badge colours:
- accepted → amber
- confirmed → blue
- deficit → red
- dispatched → green

---

**4. manager_dashboard.html**

Full-width table of all orders.

```javascript
// On load:
GET /api/manager/orders
// Returns: [{ id_order, client_name, date_created, date_delivery, status, total }]
```

Table columns: Order #, Client, Date placed, Delivery date, Status badge, Total, Actions.

Actions column: dropdown select with statuses (confirmed, dispatched) + "Save" button.

```javascript
// On save:
PATCH /api/manager/orders/:id/status
body: { status: "dispatched" }
```

Filter bar at top: filter by status (All / accepted / confirmed / deficit / dispatched), filter by date range. Filters work client-side on the loaded data, no extra API calls.

---

**5. api.js — shared API helper**

```javascript
// Base URL from window.location
// All requests include header: Authorization: Bearer <token from sessionStorage>
// On 401 response → redirect to /login
// Helper functions:
async function apiGet(path)
async function apiPost(path, body)
async function apiPatch(path, body)
```

---

**6. base.html**

Navigation bar: logo "Vittaim Order System" left, links right.
- If client: "New Order" | "My Orders" | "Logout"
- If manager: "All Orders" | "Logout"
- Role stored in sessionStorage alongside token
- Logout clears sessionStorage, calls POST /api/logout, redirects to /login

---

**Additional requirements:**
- All forms prevent default submit, use fetch() instead
- No page reloads anywhere except login/logout redirects
- Cart total recalculates on every quantity input event
- Delivery date minimum = tomorrow (set via JS on page load)
- All monetary values formatted as `X.XX BYN`
- Mobile responsive: on screens < 768px cart moves below catalogue, table scrolls horizontally
- No localStorage — use sessionStorage only
- If sessionStorage has no token on any protected page → redirect to /login immediately

**Deliver:** all 6 files complete and ready to drop into Flask project. Include inline comments explaining non-obvious logic.
</user_query>

+from __future__ import annotations

+}
+TOKENS: dict[str, dict] = {}
+
+PRODUCTS = [
+    {"id_product": 1, "name": "Milk 1L", "category": "Dairy", "unit": "pcs", "stock_qty": 50.0, "price": 2.30, "freq": 9},
+    {"id_product": 2, "name": "Cheese 200g", "category": "Dairy", "unit": "pcs", "stock_qty": 15.0, "price": 4.90, "freq": 6},
+    {"id_product": 3, "name": "Bread", "category": "Bakery", "unit": "pcs", "stock_qty": 0.0, "price": 1.60, "freq": 2},
+    {"id_product": 4, "name": "Apples", "category": "Fruits", "unit": "kg", "stock_qty": 30.0, "price": 3.40, "freq": 0},
+    {"id_product": 5, "name": "Tomatoes", "category": "Vegetables", "unit": "kg", "stock_qty": 22.0, "price": 5.10, "freq": 0},
+    {"id_product": 6, "name": "Rice", "category": "Grocery", "unit": "kg", "stock_qty": 40.0, "price": 3.80, "freq": 4},
+]
+ORDERS: list[dict] = []
+ORDER_SEQ = 1000
+
+
+def _auth_info():
+    header = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
+    if not header.startswith("Bearer "):
+        return None
+    return TOKENS.get(header[7:])
+
+
+def _require_auth(role: str | None = None):
+    def decorator(fn):
+        @wraps(fn)
+        def wrapped(*args, **kwargs):
+            auth = _auth_info()
+            if not auth:
+                return jsonify({"message": "Unauthorized"}), 401
+            if role and auth["role"] != role:
+                return jsonify({"message": "Forbidden"}), 403
+            return fn(auth, *args, **kwargs)
+
+        return wrapped
+
+    return decorator
+
+
+def _status_total(lines: list[dict]) -> float:
+    total = 0.0
+    for line in lines:
+        p = next((x for x in PRODUCTS if x["id_product"] == line["id_product"]), None)
+        if p:
+            total += float(line["quantity"]) * float(p["price"])
+    return round(total, 2)
+
+
+def register_routes(app):
+    @app.get("/")
+    def home():
+        return render_template("login.html")
+
+    @app.get("/login")
+    def login_page():
+        return render_template("login.html")
+
+    @app.get("/order")
+    def order_page():
+        return render_template("order_form.html")
+
+    @app.get("/orders")
+    def orders_page():
+        return render_template("order_history.html")
+
+    @app.get("/manager")
+    def manager_page():
+        return render_template("manager_dashboard.html")
+
+    @app.post("/api/login")
+    def login_api():
+        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
+        login = payload.get("login", "").strip()
+        password = payload.get("password", "")
+        user = USERS.get(login)
+        if not user or user["password"] != password:
+            return jsonify({"message": "Invalid credentials"}), 401
+
+        token = str(uuid4())
+        TOKENS[token] = {"login": login, "role": user["role"], "name": user["name"]}
+        return jsonify({"token": token, "role": user["role"]})
+
+    @app.post("/api/logout")
+    def logout_api():
+        auth = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
+        if auth.startswith("Bearer "):
+            TOKENS.pop(auth[7:], None)
+        return jsonify({"ok": True})
+
+    @app.get("/api/products")
+    @_require_auth()
+    def products_api(_auth):
+        sorted_products = sorted(PRODUCTS, key=lambda p: (-float(p["freq"]), p["name"].lower()))
+        return jsonify(sorted_products)
+
+    @app.get("/api/orders")
+    @_require_auth()
+    def orders_api(auth):
+        role = auth["role"]
+        login = auth["login"]
+        records = [o for o in ORDERS if role == "manager" or o["client_login"] == login]
+        return jsonify(
+            [
+                {
+                    "id_order": o["id_order"],
+                    "date_created": o["date_created"],
+                    "date_delivery": o["date_delivery"],
+                    "status": o["status"],
+                    "total": o["total"],
+                }
+                for o in records
+            ]
+        )
+
+    @app.get("/api/orders/<int:id_order>")
+    @_require_auth()
+    def order_details_api(auth, id_order):
+        order = next((o for o in ORDERS if o["id_order"] == id_order), None)
+        if not order:
+            return jsonify({"message": "Order not found"}), 404
+        if auth["role"] != "manager" and order["client_login"] != auth["login"]:
+            return jsonify({"message": "Forbidden"}), 403
+        return jsonify(order)
+
+    @app.post("/api/orders")
+    @_require_auth("client")
+    def create_order_api(auth):
+        global ORDER_SEQ
+        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
+        lines = payload.get("lines", [])
+        date_delivery = payload.get("date_delivery")
+        comment = (payload.get("comment") or "")[:500]
+
+        deficit_lines = []
+        for line in lines:
+            pid = int(line.get("id_product"))
+            qty = float(line.get("quantity", 0))
+            p = next((x for x in PRODUCTS if x["id_product"] == pid), None)
+            if not p:
+                continue
+            if qty > float(p["stock_qty"]):
+                alternatives = [
+                    {
+                        "id_product": alt["id_product"],
+                        "name": alt["name"],
+                        "stock_qty": alt["stock_qty"],
+                        "price": alt["price"],
+                    }
+                    for alt in PRODUCTS
+                    if alt["category"] == p["category"] and alt["id_product"] != p["id_product"] and alt["stock_qty"] > 0
+                ]
+                deficit_lines.append(
+                    {
+                        "id_product": pid,
+                        "name": p["name"],
+                        "requested": qty,
+                        "available": p["stock_qty"],
+                        "alternatives": alternatives,
+                    }
+                )
+
+        if deficit_lines:
+            return jsonify({"status": "deficit", "deficit_lines": deficit_lines})
+
+        for line in lines:
+            pid = int(line["id_product"])
+            qty = float(line["quantity"])
+            p = next((x for x in PRODUCTS if x["id_product"] == pid), None)
+            if p:
+                p["stock_qty"] = max(0.0, round(float(p["stock_qty"]) - qty, 2))
+
+        ORDER_SEQ += 1
+        now = date.today().isoformat()
+        total = _status_total(lines)
+        full_lines = []
+        for line in lines:
+            pid = int(line["id_product"])
+            qty = float(line["quantity"])
+            p = next((x for x in PRODUCTS if x["id_product"] == pid), None)
+            if not p:
+                continue
+            full_lines.append(
+                {
+                    "name": p["name"],
+                    "quantity": qty,
+                    "unit": p["unit"],
+                    "price_fixed": p["price"],
+                    "line_total": round(qty * float(p["price"]), 2),
+                }
+            )
+
+        ORDERS.append(
+            {
+                "id_order": ORDER_SEQ,
+                "client_name": auth["name"],
+                "client_login": auth["login"],
+                "date_created": now,
+                "date_delivery": date_delivery,
+                "status": "confirmed",
+                "total": total,
+                "comment": comment,
+                "lines": full_lines,
+                "created_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
+            }
+        )
+
+        return jsonify({"status": "confirmed", "id_order": ORDER_SEQ, "date_delivery": date_delivery})
+
+    @app.get("/api/manager/orders")
+    @_require_auth("manager")
+    def manager_orders_api(_auth):
+        return jsonify(

+                    "status": o["status"],
+                    "total": o["total"],

**Most likely cause:** nothing is accepting connections on `127.0.0.1:5000` — almost always because the Flask app **is not running**, it **exited immediately** (error on start), or it’s bound to a **different host/port** than you think.

---

### Fastest checks (in order)

1. **Confirm the server is actually running**
   - In the folder that contains `app.py`, run:

   - You should see something like `Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000`.  
   - If it prints an error and returns to the prompt, fix that first (wrong directory, missing Flask, typo, wrong Python).

2. **Wrong directory / wrong `app.py`**
   - `ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` is the same if you started a different project or never started anything. Run from the project root where **this** `app.py` and `templates/` live.

3. **Flask not installed for the Python you’re using**
   - Run:  
     `python -c "import flask; print(flask.__version__)"`  
   - If it fails:  
     `python -m pip install flask`

4. **Port 5000 is something else / already in use (less common — usually you’d see Flask fail to bind)**
   - PowerShell:  
     `netstat -ano | findstr :5000`  
   - No line with `LISTENING` ⇒ nothing listening (back to steps 1–2).

5. **Binding not on 127.0.0.1**
   - If you changed `host=` to something other than `127.0.0.1` or `localhost`, use the URL Flask prints in the terminal.

---

### Rule of thumb

- **`Connection refused`** ⇒ client reached the loopback stack but **nothing listened on that TCP port** ⇒ start or fix the server process first; rarely a browser or “cache” issue.

### Как смотреть без Flask

1. **Просто открыть HTML в браузере**  
   Двойной клик по `login.html` (или перетащить файл в вкладку).

2. **Ограничение без сервера**  
   Страницы с `extends` Jinja (`{% extends "base.html" %}`) **сами по себе** браузер не соберёт — это шаблоны для сервера. Для чистого фронта либо:
   - копировать разметку в один файл, либо
   - подставить уже «срендеренный» HTML (как Flask отдавал бы).

3. **`fetch()` к `/api/...`**  
   Без Flask (или любого backend) запросы к API **упадут** (нет сервера) или при `file://` могут вести себя иначе. Для **только визуала** формы/таблицы можно смотреть; живой вход/историю — нужен хотя бы **заглушка** или **локальный мок**.


В **`routes.py`** обновил демо-каталог под **узкую оптовую овощно-фруктовую линейку**:

- **Яблоки** — 9 позиций (Red / Golden / Granny Smith / Gala / Fuji / Braeburn / Pink Lady / Jonagold / Idared processing).
- **Прочие фрукты** — цитрус (Navel, Valencia, кровавые апельсины — **0 на остатке** для демо «недоступно»), лимоны, лаймы, грейпфрут, бананы Cavendish, груши, виноград, ягоды (лоты/короба), черешня, сливы, персики, киви, арбузы.
- **Овощи** — несколько **типов томатов и огурцов**, **перец** (зелёный/красный/жёлтый, острый микс), баклажан, кабачок, брокколи, цветная капуста, капуста, салаты (iceberg/romaine коробами), шпинат, руккола, сельдерей, **лук** (жёлтый/красный + шалот), чеснок очищенный оптом, **картофель** (russet / baby new), морковь, свёкла, шампиньоны.

Для опта добавлены **единицы** `kg`, `crt` (короб/лот), `pcs` (штуки/упаковки), в названиях — **класс, сорт, формат поставки** (wholesale box, bulk, grade).

### Что изменилось
- Удалены `app.py`, `routes.py` и Jinja-шаблоны в `templates/`

  - `index.html` → редирект на заказ

  - `manager_dashboard.html`
- Данные и логика API в **`static/js/mock-data.js`** (каталог, заказы, дефицит — всё в `sessionStorage`)
- **`static/js/api.js`** больше не ходит на сервер, вызывает `MockStore`

### Как запустить (одна команда)

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window"
python -m http.server 8080
```

Терминал **не закрывай**, пока смотришь сайт.

### Ссылки

| Страница | URL |
|----------|-----|
| Главная (заказ) | http://127.0.0.1:8080/order_form.html |
| Мои заказы | http://127.0.0.1:8080/order_history.html |
| Менеджер | http://127.0.0.1:8080/manager_dashboard.html |

Корень тоже работает: http://127.0.0.1:8080/ — перекинет на форму заказа.

Flask и `pip install` больше не нужны. Кнопка **Reset session** в шапке сбрасывает демо-данные.

[REDACTED]

[Image]
<user_query>
почему выдает это
</user_query>

**Две причины сразу:**

### 1. Сервер не запущен
`ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` / `-102` значит: на **127.0.0.1:8080** **никто не слушает**. Браузер доходит до адреса, но процесса с `python -m http.server 8080` нет (терминал закрыт, команда не выполнялась или упала).

**Что сделать:** в PowerShell:

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window"
python -m http.server 8080
```

Должно появиться `Serving HTTP on ... port 8080`. Окно **не закрывай**.

---

### 2. Старый URL — его больше нет
Ты открываешь:

`http://127.0.0.1:8080/templates/login.html`

После переписывания **без Flask**:
- папка **`templates/`** больше не используется;
- **`login.html` удалён** (логин отключён);
- страницы лежат **в корне проекта**.

**Правильные ссылки:**

| Страница | URL |
|----------|-----|
| Заказ | http://127.0.0.1:8080/order_form.html |
| Мои заказы | http://127.0.0.1:8080/order_history.html |
| Менеджер | http://127.0.0.1:8080/manager_dashboard.html |
| Главная | http://127.0.0.1:8080/ |

---

**Кратко:** сначала запусти `python -m http.server 8080`, потом открой **http://127.0.0.1:8080/order_form.html**, а не `/templates/login.html`.

Добавлю `.gitignore`, чтобы в репозиторий не попали служебные папки Cursor.

[REDACTED]

В Git нужно положить **только эти 13 файлов** (вся автономная статика, без Flask/npm):

```
empty-window/
├── .gitignore
├── index.html
├── order_form.html
├── order_history.html
├── manager_dashboard.html
└── static/
    ├── css/
    │   └── style.css
    └── js/
        ├── mock-data.js    ← каталог + заказы (мок API)
        ├── api.js
        ├── layout.js
        ├── order_form.js
        ├── order_history.js
        └── manager.js
```

**Не коммить:** `app.py`, `routes.py`, `templates/`, `.cursor/`, `mcps/`, `terminals/` — они не нужны для работы сайта.

---

### Быстрый старт Git

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window"
git init
git add .gitignore index.html order_form.html order_history.html manager_dashboard.html static/
git commit -m "Static wholesale order UI with in-browser mock API"
git remote add origin <URL-твоего-репо>
git push -u origin main
```

---

### Как будет работать онлайн

Любой **статический хостинг** (GitHub Pages, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages):

1. Корень сайта = папка с `index.html`
2. Build / npm **не нужны**
3. Данные живут в браузере (`sessionStorage` через `mock-data.js`) — **без backend**

**GitHub Pages:** Settings → Pages → Source: branch `main`, folder `/ (root)`.

Ссылки после деплоя:

- `https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/` → главная
- `https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/order_form.html`
- `https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/order_history.html`
- `https://<user>.github.io/<repo>/manager_dashboard.html`

---

**Важно:** это **демо-фронт**. Заказы и остатки хранятся только в **этом браузере**; у другого пользователя или после «Reset session» — свои данные. Для общей базы позже нужен настоящий backend API.

### Или ZIP на рабочий стол

```powershell
Compress-Archive -Path "C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\index.html","C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\order_form.html","C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\order_history.html","C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\manager_dashboard.html","C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\.gitignore","C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window\static" -DestinationPath "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop\vittaim-site.zip" -Force
```

### В Git — только эти файлы

```powershell
cd "C:\Users\Asus\.cursor\projects\empty-window"
git init
git add .gitignore index.html order_form.html order_history.html manager_dashboard.html static/
git commit -m "Static wholesale order UI"
```

Структура в репозитории должна быть **именно такой** — `index.html` в корне, рядом папка `static/`. Тогда на GitHub Pages всё откроется без сборки и без сервера.

Если нужно — могу в следующем сообщении вывести **полное содержимое каждого файла** по одному (это будет длинно, ~1500 строк).
